steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Boundaries question testing punctuation within a sentence. The sentence contains an opening em-dash after "that—" which introduces the parenthetical element "assuming solar panel installers' cooperation." The blank must close that parenthetical so that "this practice has the potential to increase..." continues as the main clause. The rule: paired dashes set off a supplementary element — the closing punctuation must match the opening dash.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'cooperation, this': A comma cannot be paired with a dash to close a parenthetical. A dash opens; a comma closes — that is a mismatch. ✗
  • Choice B — 'cooperation — this': A second dash correctly closes the parenthetical element opened by the first dash after "that." The supplementary phrase "assuming solar panel installers' cooperation" is properly bracketed, and "this practice..." resumes the main clause. ✓
  • Choice C — 'cooperation : this': A colon cannot be paired with a dash to close a parenthetical, and a colon here would incorrectly signal an elaboration or list following. ✗
  • Choice D — 'cooperation this': No punctuation leaves the parenthetical element unclosed, so the sentence structure is broken and the supplementary phrase runs into the main clause without separation. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
